22 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Chairman Crawford: The U.S. Is Not at War with Venezuela US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 22 Jan 2026 -- Today, House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) spoke on the House floor in opposition to Democrats' misguided, illogical War Powers Resolution in reference to a law enforcement mission to execute an arrest warrant on illegitimate Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro.

  • Coast Guard, CBP, Homeland Security Task Force - San Juan Region partner agencies interdict drug smuggling vessel north of Puerto Rico US Coast Guard 22 Jan 2026 -- A Coast Guard Station San Juan boat crew working with Homeland Security Task Force - San Juan Region partner agencies interdicted a drug smuggling vessel and apprehended three men in Atlantic Ocean waters north of Puerto Rico, Jan. 14, 2026.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • After Greenland Tensions, A Tentative Deal Comes Out Of Davos RFE/RL 22 Jan 2026 -- There may be a solution to the recent tension over Greenland. US President Donald Trump and NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, in a much-touted meeting held in Davos, Switzerland, on January 21, reached a framework agreement.
  • Europe remains wary as Trump cancels Greenland tariff threat Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- Though US President Donald Trump walked back threatened tariffs on eight European countries, claiming that a "framework for a future deal" on Greenland had been reached and that Washington would not use force over the island, European allies remained wary. An emergency summit of leaders of the EU's 27 member states is set to convene in Brussels late Thursday, according to multiple media reports.
  • US to Have Full Military Access to Greenland - Trump Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- The United States will have unimpeded access to Greenland as part of the deal being negotiated, President Donald Trump said on Thursday, adding that Washington will be able to deploy all the required equipment to the island.
  • Rutte Did Not Offer Trump Compromises on Greenland's Sovereignty at Davos Meeting Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said that thanks to his productive meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, a framework for a potential future agreement concerning Greenland and the broader Arctic region had been developed.
  • Trump Announces Framework of Greenland Deal after Meeting NATO's Rutte Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that the concept of the agreement on Greenland is elaborated, and opined that the future deal would be beneficial for the United States and its partners.
  • Claims of so-called 'China threat' groundless; China opposes being used as pretext to seek selfish interests, says FM spokesperson over NATO's claim on Arctic security Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- China has consistently advocated handling relations between countries in accordance with the purposes and principles of the United Nations Charter. Claims of a so-called "China threat" are entirely groundless, and China opposes fabricating issues and using China as a pretext to seek selfish interests, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Thursday. Guo made the remarks in response to a media inquiry regarding NATO spokesperson's claim on Arctic security, and meeting between NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte and US President Donald Trump.
  • Trump reportedly drops Greenland tariff threat with claim of 'framework'; ultimate outcome remains uncertain: Chinese expert Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has announced that he would not impose tariffs he had threatened to impose on eight European nations that had been set to take effect on February 1 unless those nations allowed the US to take control of Greenland, according to multiple media reports.
  • Davos becomes political battleground after Trump's Greenland threats People's Daily 22 Jan 2026 -- The World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos, traditionally a key platform for discussing global economic trends and promoting cooperative development, is evolving into a political battleground this year amid growing tensions over U.S. President Donald Trump's bid to take over Greenland.
  • Trump reiterates push to acquire Greenland, slams Europe, NATO at Davos People's Daily 22 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday renewed his push for U.S. acquisition of Greenland and sharpened criticism of Europe and the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) in a special address at the annual meeting of the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, adding fresh strain to transatlantic ties.
  • "Denmark and the people of Greenland can count on our solidarity" Germany - Federal Government 22 Jan 2026 -- Speech by the Federal Chancellor at the World Economic Forum in Davos

  • Buoyed by Venezuela interference, Trump now seeks 'regime change' in Cuba: Report Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump is reportedly preparing a new regime-change push against Cuba, less than a month after the forcible removal of Venezuela's president, relying on economic warfare and internal destabilization to break Havana's political system.
  • After Venezuela, US Now Seeks Regime Change in Cuba — Report Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- The Trump administration is actively looking for insiders within the Cuban government to strike a deal to end Communist rule, emboldened by the ouster of Nicolas Maduro and viewing it as a blueprint for Havana.

  • Joint Task Force-Bravo receives Denton Delivery in Honduras SOUTHCOM 22 Jan 2026 -- A C-5M Super Galaxy from the 433rd Airlift Wing Joint Base San Antonio-Lackland, Texas, delivered 90,000 pounds of humanitarian cargo to Soto Cano Air Base, Honduras, Jan. 12, 2026.

  • From Infrastructure to Operations: NISRF takes another step toward Full Operational Capability NATO Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance Force (NISRF) 22 Jan 2026 -- The NATO Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance Force (NISRF) continues to make tangible progress toward achieving Full Operational Capability (FOC). A key step on this path is the build-up of the new Mission Operations Support Center (MOSC) in a recently security-accredited building at the Italian Air Force Base Sigonella, Sicily.
  • NATO Secretary General discusses transatlantic security, champions defence investments and Ukraine support in Davos NATO 22 Jan 2026 -- NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte attended the World Economic Forum in Davos (20-22 January 2026), engaging in high-level discussions on global security and resilience.
  • Top NATO military authorities meet in Brussels NATO 22 Jan 2026 -- Brussels, NATO HQ, 21-22 January 2026 -- The 194th NATO Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence session took place at NATO Headquarters in Brussels. The Chair of the NATO Military Committee (CMC), Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, the 32 Allied Chiefs of Defence and the two Strategic Commanders, General Grynkewich and Admiral Vandier, met to discuss how NATO, from a military standpoint, can address the security challenges and opportunities of the current global security environment.
  • NATO-Azerbaijan relations on a solid track, as Deputy Secretary General wraps up her visit to Azerbaijan NATO 22 Jan 2026 -- On 21-22 January 2025, NATO's Deputy Secretary General, Radmila Shekerinska, visited Azerbaijan. She met Foreign Minister Jeyhun Bayramov, Speaker of the Parliament Sahiba Gafarova, and Representative of the President of the Republic of Azerbaijan on Special Assignments Elchin Amirbayov.
  • NATO Special Representative concludes his visit to Israel NATO 22 Jan 2026 -- The NATO Secretary General's Special Representative for the Southern Neighbourhood, Javier Colomina, conducted his first official visit to Israel, from 19 to 21 January 2026. He met senior officials from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ministry of Defence, and the National Security Council, with whom he discussed the regional security situation, bilateral relations, as well as Iran's destabilising activities.

  • Norway secures air defence missiles for Ukraine Government of Norway 22 Jan 2026 -- Russia's relentless missile attacks on Ukraine continue. Norway has recently delivered a significant number of air defence missiles to Ukraine to ensure that the Norwegian-developed NASAMS system can continue to protect Ukraine.
  • Serhii Sternenko appointed adviser on enhancing the use of UAVs at the frontline - Mykhailo Fedorov Ukraine MOD 22 Jan 2026
  • In 2025, more than a quarter million defenders obtained Combatant Status, with nearly half granted automatically Ukraine MOD 22 Jan 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov discussed strengthening air defence and expanding cooperation under PURL initiative with Norway's Defence Minister Ukraine MOD 22 Jan 2026
  • Government approves dismissal of five Deputy Ministers of Defence Ukraine MOD 22 Jan 2026
  • Ministry of Defence expands training of civil servants on mobilization issues: first group completes the course Ukraine MOD 22 Jan 2026

  • China's first Type 054B frigate reaches operational capability, official media reveals amid warship's first commissioning anniversary Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- China's first Type 054B frigate, the Luohe, has reached operational capability, as revealed in an official media report on the first anniversary of the warship's commissioning on Thursday. An expert said that the short period it took for the new-type vessel to become combat-ready is incredible.
  • Xi signs order to promulgate regulations on military theory work PRC MND 22 Jan 2026 -- Xi Jinping, chairman of the Central Military Commission, has signed an order to promulgate regulations on work related to military theory.

  • U.S. envoy pledges support for Taiwan defense industry, cites new ammo test range CNA 22 Jan 2026 -- Raymond Greene, the U.S. representative to Taiwan, said Thursday that Washington is committed to expanding Taiwan's domestic defense industrial base, citing a new medium-caliber ammunition test range installed in Taiwan and other initiatives to help build secure drone supply chains.

  • CDS releases Military Quantum Mission Policy Framework India PIB 22 Jan 2026 -- Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan, on January 22, 2026, released the Military Quantum Mission Policy Framework, a comprehensive document that includes the policy and the roadmap to implement Quantum Technologies in the Armed Forces
  • Kenya Defence Forces must explore collaborative opportunities with Indian companies, says Secretary (Defence Production) at 3rd India-Kenya Defence Exhibition & Seminar India PIB 22 Jan 2026 -- Secretary (Defence Production) Shri Sanjeev Kumar has urged the Kenya Defence Forces to explore collaborative opportunities with Indian companies

  • First Fire Unit Grifo Short Range Air Defence SHORAD Systems Delivered to Italian Army OCCAR 22 Jan 2026 -- The OCCAR (Organisation for Joint Armament Co-operation) FSAF-PAAMS Programme Division has announced the achievement of a significant milestone with the successful delivery of the first Fire Unit Grifo - Short‑Range Air Defence SHORAD) systems to the Esercito Italiano - Italian Army.

  • PM call with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte: 22 January 2026 Gov.UK 22 Jan 2026
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Frederiksen of Denmark: 22 January 2026 Gov.UK 22 Jan 2026
  • UK Joint Squadron deploys Typhoon jets to Qatar UK MOD 22 Jan 2026 -- 12 Squadron begins deployment to the Gulf region reflecting the strong UK-Qatar defence partnership that helps to keep the UK secure at home and strong abroad
  • Half a a billion investment to upgrade RAF Typhoons and secure 1,500 British jobs UK MOD 22 Jan 2026 -- The government has this week committed over £650 million to upgrade the RAF's Typhoon fighter jet fleet, securing over 1,500 jobs across the UK.

  • Romania has handed over command of the European Union Force in Bosnia and Herzegovina Romania - Ministry of National Defence 22 Jan 2026 -- State Secretary for Defence Policy, Planning and International Relations, Sorin-Dan Moldovan, and the Deputy Chief of Defence Staff for Operations and Training, Lieutenant General Iulian Berdilă, participated today, January 21st, in Camp Butmir, Sarajevo, in the command handover/takeover ceremony of the European Union Force in Bosnia and Herzegovina (EUFOR ALTHEA), the largest multinational operation in the Western Balkans, conducted under the aegis of the European Union's Common Security and Defence Policy.
  • General Vlad's participation in NATO Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session Romania - Ministry of National Defence 22 Jan 2026 -- The Chief of Defence Staff, General Vlad Gheorghiță, participates, between January 21st and 22nd, 2026, in the NATO Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session (MCCS), organized at NATO headquarters, in Brussels.

  • Navy boosts reach with new ships as Army modernizes tactics Philippine News Agency 22 Jan 2026 -- The country's new fleet of modern naval ships is expected to enhance the Philippine Navy's (PN) reach and patrol to protect the country's vast maritime domain, its spokesperson said on Thursday.

  • United States Conducts Inspections in Antarctica US Dept. of State 22 Jan 2026-- A team of U.S. government officials from the Department of State, National Science Foundation, and Department of War conducted a five-day inspection of foreign research stations, installations, and equipment in Antarctica from January 16 to January 20.
  • Joint Statement by Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. US Dept. of State 22 Jan 2026 -- Termination of U.S. Membership in the World Health Organization (WHO)

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Jan. 22, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 22 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 22 Jan 2026
  • 'Dangerous nostalgia' is a threat to multilateralism, UN deputy chief tells Danish MPs UN News 22 Jan 2026 -- United Nations Deputy Secretary-General Amina Mohammed urged countries to protect the UN Charter in a powerful speech to the Danish Parliament on Thursday, urging countries to "stand up for a rules-based order" - or pay the price.
  • Deputy Secretary-General's remarks to the Danish Parliament: "The Future of the United Nations in a Turbulent World" [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 22 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israel Fails to Pull Troops From Gaza in 1st Phase of Peace Deal - Palestinian Official Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- Israel has failed to complete its obligations to withdraw troops from the Gaza Strip under the first phase of US President Donald Trump's peace plan for the Palestinian enclave, retaining its presence on 60% of newly occupied Palestinian territories, Palestinian General Supervisor of Official Media Ahmad Assaf told Sputnik on Thursday.

  • The Chief of the General Staff in the Central Command Israel Defense Forces 22 Jan 2026
  • Three Hideouts and Weapons Found in an Underground Tunnel: the Kfir Brigade and the Yahalom Unit Dismantle a 1 Kilometer Tunnel Route Israel Defense Forces 22 Jan 2026

  • Israeli minister authorizes gun permits for illegal settlers in occupied West Bank Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- Far-right Israeli minister Itamar Ben-Gvir has authorized personal firearm permits for residents of 18 more illegal West Bank settlements amid growing settler violence aimed at displacing Palestinians.

  • Israel Reports Striking Four Syria-Lebanon Border Crossings Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) has reported that it struck four border crossings between Syria and Lebanon, which it said were used by the Lebanon-based Hezbollah movement to smuggle weapons.

  • President Trump Ratifies Board of Peace in Historic Ceremony, Opening Path to Hope and Dignity for Gazans The White House 22 Jan 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Board of Peace Charter Signing Ceremony US Dept. of State 22 Jan 2026
  • Trump Launches Board Of Peace, Putin Seeks To Use Frozen Assets For Sign-Up Fee RFE/RL 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump launched the Board of Peace, a new body for resolving international conflicts, repeating his belief that a peace deal to end Russia's invasion of Ukraine "is coming very soon."
  • Explainer: Trump Signs 'Board Of Peace' Into Existence RFE/RL 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has signed into existence a Board of Peace, an initiative he says will seek to resolve global conflicts and promote stability, peace, and governance "in areas affected or threatened by conflict."
  • Trump launches 'Board of Peace' seen as bid to control Gaza Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has launched a new "Board of Peace" under the banner of supporting ceasefire in the besieged Gaza Strip, a move mostly seen as an attempt to weaken the United Nations and replace multilateral diplomacy with US-dominated decision-making.

  • Nineteen States Sign Charter of US-Formed Board of Peace for Gaza in Davos Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- The charter of the US-formed Board of Peace for Gaza in Davos was signed by 19 countries and the self-proclaimed Kosovo, according to the broadcast of the event on Thursday.
  • Upon Instruction of His Majesty King Mohammed VI, may God assist Him, Chairman of the Al Quds Committee, Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates Mr. Nasser Bourita proceeded to sign on Thursday in Davos, Switzerland the founding Charter of the Board of Peace, during a ceremony chaired by President of the United States of America H.E. Donald J. Trump. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Jan 2026
  • Foreign Minister Signs Board of Peace Founding Charter SPA 22 Jan 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah signed today the founding charter of the Board of Peace in the presence of President Donald Trump of the United States of America and leaders and representatives of a number of countries, welcoming the establishment of the board. The signing took place during the board's launch ceremony on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum (WEF) Annual Meeting 2026 in Davos, Switzerland
  • Prime Minister Rossen Jeliazkov: United States and international community duly appreciate Bulgaria's accession to Board of Peace Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria 22 Jan 2026

  • Chinese FM responds to reports of Trump holding signing ceremony for 'Board of Peace' in Davos, says no additional information Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- When asked to comment on reports that US President Donald Trump will hold a signing ceremony for the so-called Board of Peace in Davos, Switzerland, later Thursday, and that about 35 of the 50 invited leaders have accepted the invitation — as well as whether China is considering attending — Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said that China has already responded to questions regarding the "Board of Peace" and currently has no additional information to provide.

  • Recreational supplies get into Gaza after two years - UNICEF UNICEF 22 Jan 2026 -- After more than two years of restrictions, UNICEF has been allowed to bring recreational kits to the Gaza Strip to support children's learning, wellbeing, and resilience. Since Thursday 15 January, 5,168 recreational kits have entered the Strip, supporting more than 375,000 children - including 1,000 children with disabilities.
  • Gaza: War crimes probe pledges to continue work for justice and accountability UN News 22 Jan 2026 -- As President Trump launched the international Board of Peace plan for Gaza on Thursday, top independent rights experts tasked by the UN Human Rights Council with investigating grave abuses linked to the Hamas-Israel war pledged to continue their work seeking justice and accountability for all.
  • Israel's continuous assaults on Birzeit University are an attack on education itself: UN experts OHCHR 22 Jan 2026 -- Israel's military assault on Birzeit University in the occupied West Bank is not episodic violence but a systematic policy, UN experts* said today.

  • Iran condemns Israeli demolition of UNRWA headquarters in occupied al-Quds Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- Iran has strongly condemned the Israeli regime's attack on the headquarters of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA) in occupied al-Quds and the destruction of buildings belonging to the UN body.

  • Russian-Palestinian talks President of Russia 22 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held talks with President of the State of Palestine Mahmoud Abbas, who has come to Russia on a working visit.
  • Putin Praises 'Special, Deep-Rooted' Russia-Palestine Ties Sputnik 22 Jan 2026 -- Relations between Russia and Palestine have deep roots and are of a special nature, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, South Sudan, Venezuela, Mozambique UNOCHA 22 Jan 2026

  • Coalition to Restore Legitimacy in Yemen Condemns Attack on Giants Brigades Commander, Vows 'Iron Fist' Response to Destabilization Attempts SPA 22 Jan 2026 -- Official Spokesperson of the Coalition to Restore Legitimacy in Yemen Major General Turki Al-Malki stated that the coalition strongly condemns the cowardly terrorist attack targeting the convoy of Brigadier General Hamdi Shukri, Commander of the Second Division of the Giants Brigades, and his companions. The attack, which took place this evening in the Jaoula area of Lahj Governorate, resulted in the martyrdom of several people and the wounding of others

  • The United States Applauds Iraqi Leadership on Countering ISIS US Dept. of State 22 Jan 2026

  • Syria: Renewed clashes risk derailing fragile transition UN News 22 Jan 2026 -- A tense standoff between the Syrian Government and Kurdish-led forces in the country's northeast risks igniting wider instability just as Syria struggles to emerge from years of conflict and authoritarian rule, senior UN officials told the Security Council on Thursday.
  • Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Syria by Ms. Edem Wosornu, Director, Crisis Response Division, OCHA, on behalf of Tom Fletcher, USG and Emergency Relief Coordinator UNOCHA 22 Jan 2026
  • Situation in Syria Still 'Very Tense', Top Political, Peacebuilding Official Tells Security Council, Reviewing Progress towards Ceasefire, National Integration UN Security Council 22 Jan 2026-- The situation in Syria remains "very tense with exchanges of fire", a senior United Nations political and peacebuilding official said today as he briefed the Security Council on progress in halting the fighting and ensuring the full integration of all three north-east governorates under central Government control.
  • Chinese FM responds to Syrian official claim that China has not requested repatriation of ETIM militants Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- The ETIM is a terrorist organization designated by China and listed by the UN Security Council in accordance with the law, and Syria has committed to not allowing any entity to use Syrian territory to engage in activities that undermine China's security, sovereignty and interests, said Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun.
  • The UK is deeply concerned about events in north-east Syria: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 22 Jan 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Syria.

  • Secretary Lazaro Hosts Myanmar Stakeholders' Meeting in Tagaytay Philippines DFA 22 Jan 2026 -- Secretary of Foreign Affairs Ma. Theresa P. Lazaro, in her capacity as the Special Envoy of the ASEAN Chair on Myanmar, hosted a Myanmar Stakeholders' Meeting in Tagaytay earlier this month, the first under the Philippines' Chairship of ASEAN.

  • PRESS RELEASE: Government of Nigeria, humanitarian partners launch urgent appeal for US$516 million to provide lifesaving support to 2.5 million people in north-east Nigeria in 2026 UNOCHA 22 Jan 2026 -- The Government of Nigeria and humanitarian partners are urgently appealing for $516 million to respond to the most critical needs of 2.5 million people in Borno, Adamawa and Yobe (BAY) States in north-east Nigeria this year. Women and children make up 8 out of every 10 people in immediate need.
  • WFP warns of imminent food assistance cuts in Nigeria as violence and hunger surges across the north WFP 22 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is sounding the alarm as more than one million people in northeast Nigeria risk being cut off from emergency food and nutrition assistance within weeks unless urgent new funding is received. As a result, and for the first time in Nigeria, WFP's assistance will be limited to only 72,000 people.

News Reports

  • Maryland Man Indicted on Unlawful Transmission and Retention of Classified National Defense Information Charges US Dept. of Justice 22 Jan 2026 -- Today, a federal grand jury indicted a Laurel, Maryland man on five counts of unlawfully transmitting and one count of unlawfully retaining classified national defense information, in violation of 18 U.S.C. § 793(e).

  • Treasury Sanctions One of the Caribbean's Largest Narcotics Traffickers and His Network US Treasury 22 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated five Costa Rican nationals and five Costa Rica-based entities for their involvement in narcotics trafficking and money laundering.

  • Meeting on the microelectronics industry President of Russia 22 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting on expanding domestic integrated electronics.
  • Meeting with US President's Special Envoy Steve Witkoff President of Russia 22 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin met with Special Envoy of the President of the United States of America Steve Witkoff.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to a question from Rossiya Segodnya about the situation involving Russian crew members of the Marinera oil tanker seized by the US authorities Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Jan 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister of Belarus Maxim Ryzhenkov Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Jan 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on January 22, 2026 PRC MofA 22 Jan 2026
  • UN experts alarmed by reports of forced labour of Uyghur, Tibetan and other minorities across China OHCHR 22 Jan 2026 -- UN experts* today expressed deep concern regarding persistent allegations of forced labour affecting Uyghur, Kazakh and Kyrgyz minority groups as well as Tibetans within the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region and across other parts of China.
  • China's first full-scale commercial crewed spacecraft test capsule unveiled, opening new chapter in space economy Global Times 22 Jan 2026 -- InterstellOr, the only private company in China approved to undertake a national-level commercial crewed spaceflight project, on Thursday unveiled China's first full-scale commercial crewed spacecraft test capsule, Suborbital Spacecraft Crosser 1 (hereafter CYZ1), at a launch event in Chengdu, Southwest China's Sichuan Province. The CYZ1 technical team revealed that more than three spacecraft have already been booked, involving over 20 space tourists in total. The company expects to conducts its first crewed flight in 2028.

  • Taiwan's leader Lai absent from first legislative review meeting of his impeachment People's Daily 22 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's legislature on Wednesday held a plenary meeting to review the details of an impeachment motion against the island's leader, Lai Ching-te, who had been invited to attend and provide explanations but did not appear at the meeting.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 22 Jan 2026

  • Republic of Korea: High Representative/Vice-President Kallas holds second Strategic Dialogue with Foreign Minister Cho in Brussels European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 Jan 2026 -- The High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy of the European Union/European Commission Vice-President Kaja Kallas and the Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Korea (RoK) Cho Hyun held the second EU-RoK Strategic Dialogue in Brussels on 22 January.

  • EU To Target Iranian Minister, Security Chiefs Over Deadly Crackdown On Protesters RFE/RL 22 Jan 2026 -- The European Union has proposed sanctions against Iran's interior minister and 14 other senior officials for their role in a violent crackdown on nationwide protests that erupted in late December, documents obtained by RFE/RL show.
  • Senior general vows swift response to any aggression on Iran Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- An Iranian military commander has warned that any attack on the country's soil will be met with a swift, precise, and devastating response following recent threats by US President Donald Trump to strike the country.
  • 'Hands on trigger': IRGC chief warns US and Israel against any miscalculation Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- The chief commander of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned enemies against any miscalculation, stressing that his forces have "their hands on the trigger" to implement the orders of Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei.
  • Senior cmdr. strongly warns Trump following rhetorical remarks concerning Leader Press TV 22 Jan 2026 -- A senior Iranian military commander has strongly warned Donald Trump following the US president's rhetorical remarks concerning Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei.

  • Informal meeting of the members of the European Council: doorstep remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 Jan 2026 -- Check against delivery!
  • Statement by President von der Leyen with President Costa following the informal meeting of the members of the European Council of 22 January 2026 European Commission 22 Jan 2026

  • OSCE Secretary General highlights dialogue and co-operation as an answer to a polarized world at the 2026 Davos Forum OSCE 22 Jan 2026 -- OSCE Secretary General Feridun H. Sinirlioğlu concluded his participation in the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, where he highlighted the importance of dialogue and co-operation in addressing an increasingly polarized global environment.

  • The Daily Press Briefing (01/22/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 22 Jan 2026

  • Albares defends Europe's sovereignty in Davos Spain MFA 22 Jan 2026-- The minister reiterates that the principle of equality and territorial integrity of States is 'non-negotiable' and appeals to the UN Charter

  • Speech by Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul at the German Embassy in Addis Ababa Germany Federal Foreign Office 22 Jan 2026

  • Prime Minister Narendra Modi receives a telephone call from the President of Brazil (January 22, 2025) India MEA 22 Jan 2026-- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi received a telephone call today from the President of the Federative Republic of Brazil, His Excellency Mr. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

  • Davos Conference 2026 Ministry of Defense of Japan 22 Jan 2026

  • PM Pashinyan holds brief meetings in Davos Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 22 Jan 2026-- During his brief visit to Davos, the Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan held meetings with international partners and representatives of the technology sector.
  • Prime Minister Pashinyan participates in the signing ceremony of the Charter of the "Board of Peace" in Davos Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 22 Jan 2026-- Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, at the invitation of US President Donald Trump, participated in the solemn ceremony of signing the Charter of the "Board of Peace" as a founding member in Davos, within the framework of the World Economic Forum.
  • Second Meeting of Armenia-U.S. Bilateral Working Group Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 22 Jan 2026 -- On January 22, the second meeting of Armenia-U.S. Bilateral Working Group in Support of the Outcomes of the August 8, 2025 Peace Summit was held.

  • IOM Scales Up Emergency Response as Floods Displace Over Half a Million People in Mozambique IOM 22 Jan 2026 -- The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is scaling up its emergency response in Mozambique as severe flooding continues to displace communities across the country, expanding life-saving assistance and strengthening nationwide displacement tracking, coordination, and protection efforts.
  • World News in Brief: Winter attacks in Ukraine, looming food aid cuts in Nigeria, drought in Kenya UN News 22 Jan 2026 -- Russian forces launched a fresh wave of strikes across Ukraine this week which have left hundreds of thousands of families without electricity and heating amid freezing temperatures.
